c) Les principaux arguments relatifs à la demande de radiation, formulés à l'intention du Comité á partir de l'analyse de toutes les informations dont dispose il et de ses observations.
items_en: 5. At the end of the information-gathering period, the Ombudsman shall open a two-month consultation period during which the dialogue may be initiated with the applicant. Having duly examined requests for additional time, he may extend this period once, for a maximum of two months, if he considers that more time is required to conduct the dialogue and to prepare the comprehensive report described in paragraph 7 below.
6. During the consultation period, the Ombudsman:

(a) May ask the complainant questions or request him to provide additional information or clarifications that may assist the Committee in its consideration of the delisting request, and may address any questions or requests for information received from the States concerned, the Committee and the Monitoring Team;

(b) Forward the replies received from the complainant to the States concerned, the Committee and the Monitoring Team and contact the complainant with regard to the incomplete replies provided by the complainant;
(c) Ensure coordination with the States concerned, the Committee and the Monitoring Team for any further information requested by the complainant or any response to be sent to him.

7. At the end of the consultation period referred to above, the Ombudsman shall prepare and communicate to the Committee, with the assistance of the Monitoring Team, a comprehensive report containing exclusively:

(a) A summary of all information available to it regarding the delisting request, in which it provides, where appropriate, an indication of the sources, respecting the confidentiality of some of the elements communicated to it by Member States;
(b) A statement of his views on the delisting request, including the dialogue with the complainant;
(c) The main arguments relating to the delisting request made to the Committee on the basis of the analysis of all the information available to it and its observations.

text_fr: Examen de la demande et décision du Comité (deux mois)
text_en: Consideration of the submission and decision of the Committee (two months)

items_fr: 8. Lorsque le Comité a eu trente jours pour examiner le rapport d'ensemble, son président inscrit la demande de radiation à son ordre du jour.

9. Lorsque le Comité examine la demande de radiation, le Médiateur présente luimême le rapport, au besoin avec le concours de l'Équipe de surveillance, et répond aux questions posées par les membres du Comité au sujet de la demande.
10. Le Comité décide, à l'issue de l'examen, s'il approuve la demande de radiation suivant ses procédures de décision normales.

11. Si le Comité décide d'accéder à la demande de radiation, il en informe le Médiateur. Celui-ci informe à son tour le requérant de la décision, et le nom de l'intéressé est radié de la Liste récapitulative.

12. Si  le  Comité  décide  de  rejeter  la  demande  de  radiation,  il  en  informe  le Médiateur  en  lui  communiquant,  le  cas  échéant,  des  explications  et  toute  autre information utile concernant sa décision, ainsi qu'un résumé actualisé des motifs ayant présidé à l'inscription de l'intéressé sur la Liste.

13. Après que le Comité l'a informé de sa décision de rejeter la demande de radiation, le Médiateur adresse au requérant, dans un délai de 15 jours, une lettre dont il a déjà communiqué le texte au Comité, dans laquelle :
a) Il l'informe que le Comité a décidé de maintenir son inscription sur la Liste;
b) Il décrit, autant que possible et en s'inspirant du rapport d'ensemble, la procédure et les éléments d'information factuels qu'il a recueillis et qui peuvent être divulgués;
c) Il communique toutes autres informations que le Comité lui a fournies au sujet de sa décision en application du paragraphe 12 ci-dessus.
14. Dans toutes les communications avec le requérant, le Médiateur respecte le caractère confidentiel des délibérations du Comité et de ses propres communications avec les États Membres.
items_en: 8. When the Committee has had 30 days to consider the comprehensive report, its Chairperson shall place the request for de-listing on its agenda.

9. When the Committee considers the request for delisting, the Ombudsman himself shall submit the report, if necessary with the assistance of the Monitoring Team, and shall respond to questions posed by the members of the Committee concerning the request.
10. The Committee shall decide, upon completion of the review, whether to approve the delisting request in accordance with its normal decision-making procedures.

11. If the Committee decides to accede to the request for delisting, it shall inform the Ombudsman, who shall inform the complainant of the decision, and the name of the person concerned shall be removed from the Consolidated List.

12. If the Committee decides to reject the request for delisting, it shall inform the Ombudsman accordingly, providing explanations and any other relevant information concerning its decision, as well as an up-to-date summary of the reasons for listing the person concerned.

13. After the Committee has informed him of its decision to reject the request for delisting, the Ombudsman shall, within 15 days, send the complainant a letter, the text of which he has already communicated to the Committee, in which:
(a) To inform the Committee that the Committee has decided to continue its listing;
(b) It shall describe, as far as possible and on the basis of the comprehensive report, the procedure and factual information that it has collected and that may be disclosed;
(c) It shall provide any other information provided by the Committee with regard to its decision pursuant to paragraph 12 above.
14. In all communications with the complainant, the Ombudsman respects the confidentiality of the Committee ' s deliberations and its own communications with Member States.

text_fr: Autres fonctions du Bureau du Médiateur
text_en: Other functions of the Office of the Ombudsman

items_fr: 15. Outre les tâches définies ci-dessus, le Médiateur :

a) Communique à toute personne qui en fait la demande des informations qui peuvent être rendues publiques concernant les procédures du Comité, y compris les directives du Comité, les fiches d'information et les autres documents établis par le Comité;

b) Informe les personnes ou entités de leur inscription sur la Liste lorsque leur adresse est connue, après que le Secrétariat a officiellement informé la Mission permanente de l'État ou des États, conformément au paragraphe 18 de la présente résolution;
c) Présente au Conseil de sécurité des rapports semestriels sur ses activités.
items_en: 15. In addition to the tasks defined above, the Ombudsman:

(a) Provide any person who so requests with information that may be made public about the Committee's procedures, including the Committee's guidelines, fact sheets and other documents prepared by the Committee;

(b) Inform individuals or entities of their listing when their addresses are known, after the Secretariat has officially informed the Permanent Mission of the State or States, in accordance with paragraph 18 of the present resolution;
(c) To submit semi-annual reports on its activities to the Security Council.
